# Sync @neo-opus-grace handle into the resources/content archive mirror

## Context

PR #13410 merged the `@neo-claude-opus` → `@neo-opus-grace` rename (#13402) across core source, CI allowlists, rosters, and the seed-coupled unit specs — but deliberately scoped **out** the `resources/content` mirror (the data-sync domain, §critical_gate #3). GitHub auto-switched ticket/PR authorship on the username change; the local `resources/content` md (which the portal renders) does **not**, and the incremental sync pipeline never re-touches the frozen `v13.0.0` archive. So the old handle persists across 545 archived files until a one-time backfill.

This is the deliberate second half of the 2-PR split: #13410 = source/specs (merged), this = the content mirror.

## The Fix

One-time handle swap `@neo-claude-opus` → `@neo-opus-grace` across the 545 affected `resources/content` files. Committed on `agent/sync-grace-rename` (`525906340`): **2628/2628 symmetric** (no net content change), on the sanctioned `agent/sync-*` data-sync branch, `--no-verify` for pre-existing verbatim-mirror trailing whitespace (the same bypass `check-chore-sync.mjs` documents and the hourly pipeline uses).

## Architectural Reality

- `resources/content/**` — the portal's content mirror; data-sync domain (§critical_gate #3).
- `buildScripts/util/check-chore-sync.mjs` — gates `resources/content` commits to `chore/sync-*` / `agent/sync-*` branches.
- The `v13.0.0` archive is frozen; the incremental `chore(data): Hourly...` pipeline syncs recent content only, so it never self-heals the archive.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] No `neo-claude-opus` remains in `resources/content/**` (handle fully migrated to `neo-opus-grace`).
- [ ] Diff is the handle swap only — symmetric, no net content change (`2628/2628`).
- [ ] Lands on dev via `agent/sync-grace-rename`.

## Note

The mirror reflects the current identity (`grace`) uniformly, **including historical ticket/PR-title text** where the old handle appears. Intentional: the rename is retroactive to the same entity and portal identity-consistency is the goal. (GitHub keeps literal historical titles but auto-switches authorship; the mirror is aligned to the current handle, not re-fetched.)

## Out of Scope

- Re-syncing / regenerating content from GitHub (this is a targeted handle swap, not a re-sync).
- Non-`resources/content` surfaces (delivered by #13410).

## Related

- Tail of #13402 (rename); core delivered by #13410 (merged).

## Release classification

post-release (rename follow-through; non-blocking). Boardless.
